title: CI Tradecraft Chamber

fields.vision: Completes the three-pass handoff sequence for the proposed Counterintelligence Tradecraft Chamber as a Red Team Lab subspace (not a new top-level room) inside MuranoOS.
fields.mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
fields.built_components: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.source_utilization: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations. Mechanism: Tie claims to collection, covert action, deception, interrogation, bias-control, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize Capstone, SATS, CI, RAG integrity, and analyst-assurance pages for close-reading. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need method-passage extraction and claim-to-source linkage. Source pool: 60 deduplicated local title candidates; 63 catalogue support rows. Examples: Improving Intelligence Analysis; Leading Intelligence Analysis. Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Intended to guide: (1) exact Phase I-to-II conversion inside real MuranoOS protocol flow, (2) Build Planning against the live repo, (3) subspace integration inside Red Team Lab. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None. Package is repo-aware conceptual handoff. Next tranche (file 10) defines the Phase I-to-II conversion plan against the live MuranoOS protocol flow.
fields.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Completes the three-pass handoff sequence for the proposed Counterintelligence Tradecraft Chamber as a Red Team Lab subspace (not a new top-level room) inside MuranoOS. Pass 1: archive/reality audit — separated live implementation state / preserved canon / forward vNext proposal. - Pass 2: room identity and boundary lock — established the subspace identity. - Pass 3 (this package): final implementation-ready handoff — subspace identity + scope, Phase I conceptual spec, feature tiers (MVP/V2/Deferred), data entities and behavioral rules, lifecycle, integration surface, cross-cutting system assumptions, acceptance checklist.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: Completes the three-pass handoff sequence for the proposed Counterintelligence Tradecraft Chamber as a Red Team Lab subspace (not a new top-level room) inside MuranoOS.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It preserves adversarial review machinery: assumptions, vulnerabilities, rival interpretations, replay evidence, and acceptance gates can be inspected before a claim advances.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
